title: Star Atlas Crew Adventure Series – Season Production
brief: The Star Atlas Crew Adventure Series is an ongoing cinematic storytelling project that produces AI content rooted directly in Star Atlas lore.
description:
# 1. HEADER

## Star Atlas Crew Adventure Series – Season Production 
* Author: Hammerhead 
* Type: Ecosystem Fund 
* Date: November 2025 

___
 
# 2. SUMMARY

* The Star Atlas Crew Adventure Series is an ongoing cinematic storytelling project that produces AI content rooted directly in Star Atlas lore. This proposal funds the production of one complete season, consisting of: 
   * 2 months of weekly episodes (4 minutes or longer each). 
   * 3 weekly YouTube Shorts tied to each episode.  
* The request of 13,143,639 ATLAS covers the entire content pipeline: writing, worldbuilding, environment creation, character development, animation, sound design, original music, editing, and distribution on YouTube and X. 
* Earlier seasons (1–3) are already publicly released, demonstrating both capability and viewer interest. This proposal provides the resources for the next season, allowing the series to continue expanding the Star Atlas universe and offering consistent, lore-driven media that can attract new participants to the ecosystem. 
* https://www.youtube.com/@StarAtlasCrewAdventures 

___
  
# 3. MOTIVATION

* People need something to connect with; the Star Atlas series will bring people to the game as they start to connect with the characters. It will build up the anticipation for the next episode and build a hunger to get into the game and experience what the game has to offer.

---

# 4. SPECIFICATION

## 4.1 Definitions 
* DAO: The Star Atlas DAO. 
* Series: The Star Atlas Crew Adventure Series. 
* Season: A 2-month production and publishing cycle of full episodes and Shorts. 
* Episode: A 4 minute or longer narrative installment released weekly. 
* Shorts: Three companion clips per week tied to each episode. 
* Author: Hammerhead, responsible for writing, production, Episodes, and music. 

## 4.2 Program Structure 
* Each funded season includes: 
* Seven episodes (one per week over 2 months). 
* 3 Shorts per week for 7 to 8 weeks. 

## 4.3 Production Workflow
* The creator conducts the full end-to-end process: 
* Writing & Story Development 
* Season-level story arc following Star Atlas lore. 
* Episode scripts are broken into modular scenes. 
* Worldbuilding & Asset Creation 
* Custom environments: ship interiors, rooms, planet side locations. 
* New characters for each season, each with a defined personality, history, behavior patterns, and speaking style. 
* Visual continuity across seasons to support long-term narrative arcs. 
* Videos & Editing 
* Scene assembly  
* Editing in DaVinci Resolve. 
* Addition of ambience, sound effects, and original music Production. 
* All the music is made with AI by the author. 
* Music videos produced during season break. 
* Quality Review & Refinement 
* Final review to adjust pacing, visuals, and audio. 
* Ensure consistency with Star Atlas lore and thematic guidelines. 

## 4.4 AI Software Used 
* I’m using 6 different software to make the episodes:  
1. Flow VEO 3 for the visual story 
2. Google Gemini to build the set the team is in 
3. OpenArt for transition effects 
4. Eleven Labs for the character voices 
5. Suno for episodes music 
6. DaVinci Resolve for editing

## 4.5 Publication Requirements 
* Episodes and Shorts are released on YouTube. 
* Announcements and cross-posting on X.com. 
* All material adheres to Star Atlas lore and respect IP integrity. 
* Consistent weekly publishing episodes during production months. 

## 4.6 Safeguards 
* Content must remain consistent with established Star Atlas lore. 
* Episodes will maintain an approximate length of 4 minutes each or longer. 
* Shorts should be approximately 1 minute each or longer. 
* All music must be original and produced by the author. 
* No conflicts of interest are identified. 

___

# 5. STAKEHOLDER IMPACT

## 5.1 Positive Impacts 
For the Community: 
* A consistent, high-quality narrative series that deepens emotional investment. 
* Weekly entertainment cycles increase engagement and anticipation. 
* Expanded understanding of the Star Atlas universe through characters and stories.

For New Players: 
* A compelling entry point into the world of Star Atlas. 
* Clearer connection to lore, factions, environments, and themes. 

For the DAO & Ecosystem: 
* Enhanced visibility and shareable content for social platforms. 
* Strengthened brand identity and worldbuilding. 
* Long-term narrative infrastructure supports player retention and marketing efforts. 

## 5.2 Potential Drawbacks 
* The treasury commits 11,204,716 ATLAS for a single season. 
* Production is largely driven by one creator, which centralizes capability but also ensures style continuity. 
* No operational burden on the DAO aside from funding. 
* The burning of credits to make an episode can become more expensive than I wanted it to be, when AI quality is not consistent.
---

# 6. IMPLEMENTATION DETAILS

## 6.1 Oversight 
* The author is responsible for meeting the expected cadence and content structure. 
* Final episodes, Shorts, and music videos must be publicly accessible. 

# 6.2 Implementation Steps 
* Since production is ongoing, the next season follows this sequence: 
* Finalize season storyline and episode scripts. 
* Create new environments, character models, and seasonal backdrops. 
* Animate, assemble, and edit scenes for each episode. 
* Produce weekly Shorts derived from the main episode. 
* Publish one full episode per week for 2 months. 

# 6.3 Dependencies 
* Stable access to AI tools and DaVinci Resolve. 
* Continued adherence to Star Atlas Lore. 
* Creator availability and production time. 

___

# 7. TIMELINE 

* The show is already running; new episodes are ready to be released weekly.  
* There are NO delays for production. 
* The project is produced and made by me; there are two other people that help me with sounds and story line editing.  

**Season 4 will have 7 episodes and 21 Shorts dispersed in a 7 week run. Every single week you’ll get 3 Shorts and 1 Full episode.**
   * 1/26/26 S4 E1 Part 1 Short 
   * 1/28/26 S4 E1 Part 2 Short 
   * 1/30/26 S4 E1 Part 3 Short and S4 E1 (Full Episode) 
   * 2/2/26 S4 E2 Part 1 Short 
   * 2/4/26 S4 E2 Part 2 Short 
   * 2/6/26 S4 E2 Part 3 Short and S4 E2 (Full Episode) 
   * 2/9/26 S4 E3 Part 1 Short 
   * 2/11/26 S4 E3 Part 2 Short 
   * 2/13/26 S4 E3 Part 3 Short and S4 E3 (Full Episode) 
   * 2/16/26 S4 E4 Part 1 Short 
   * 2/18/26 S4 E4 Part 2 Short 
   * 2/20/26 S4 E4 Part 3 Short and S4 E4 (Full Episode) 
   * 2/23/26 S4 E5 Part 1 Short 
   * 2/25/26 S4 E5 Part 2 Short 
   * 2/27/26 S4 E5 Part 3 Short and S4 E5 (Full Episode) 
   * 3/2/26 S4 E6 Part 1 Short 
   * 3/4/26 S4 E6 Part 2 Short 
   * 3/6/26 S4 E6 Part 3 Short and S4 E6 (Full Episode) 
   * 3/9/26 S4 E7 Part 1 Short 
   * 3/11/26 S4 E7 Part 2 Short 
   * 3/13/26 S4 E7 Part 3 Short and S4 E7 (Full Episode) 

**Payment will be split into 3 parts:**
1. On 2/16/2026 in the amount of 3,285,909 ATLAS
2. On 3/13/2026 in the amount of 4,928,865 ATLAS 
3. On 4/03/2026 in the amount of 4,928,865 ATLAS

---
# 8. COST & RESOURCES ALLOCATION 

## 8.1 One-Time Costs  
* DaVinci Resolve Studio: $295 

## 8.2 Ongoing Costs 
* Flow Veo 3: $19.99 per month 
* Extra Credits: $100.00 per month 
* Eleven Labs: $22.00 per month 
* Suno: $8.00 Per Month 
* Open Art: $7.00 Per Month 
* Writing, Producing, Sounds, Music $$$ what’s left of Remaining Balance 

## 8.3 Total Requested Cost
* Max 5% Ecosystem Limit of 13,143,639 ATLAS

___

# 9. ADDITIONAL DETAILS 

* There is no conflict of interest with current or earlier versions.
